name: Triple Sloths v2
description: Three-channel chaos source with Torpor, Apathy and Inertia sections, CV-influenced slow motion, and a bottom-row difference rectifier.

controls.name: Torpor
controls.description: Biases the Torpor chaos circuit toward one attractor or the other.

controls.name: Apathy
controls.description: Biases the Apathy chaos circuit toward one attractor or the other.

inputs.name: Torpor CV
inputs.description: CV input for the Torpor chaos circuit.

inputs.name: Apathy CV
inputs.description: CV input for the Apathy chaos circuit.

outputs.name: Torpor X/Y/Z
outputs.description: Three related Torpor outputs; Z is the inverted version of Y.

outputs.name: Apathy X/Y/Z
outputs.description: Three related Apathy outputs; Z is the inverted version of Y.

outputs.name: Inertia X/Y/Z
outputs.description: Three related Inertia outputs; the section has no control and moves at the slowest rate.
Positive difference rectifier output formed from the three Z outputs.
Negative difference rectifier output formed from the three Z outputs.

behaviors.name: Three time scales
behaviors.description: Torpor moves in tens of seconds, Apathy in about a minute, and Inertia on the scale of many minutes.

behaviors.name: Weight not frequency
behaviors.trigger: turn the Apathy or Torpor pots
behaviors.description: The pots bias the output toward one attractor or the other rather than acting as simple speed controls.

behaviors.name: CV injection
behaviors.trigger: patch into Apathy CV or Torpor CV
behaviors.description: External CV is injected into the chaos circuits and can produce periodic windows or sudden voltage jumps.

behaviors.name: No Inertia control
behaviors.description: The Inertia section has no front-panel control and simply continues its own chaotic drift.

behaviors.name: Z is inverted Y
behaviors.trigger: compare any section's Y and Z outputs
behaviors.description: Each Z output is the inverted version of Y, so the section can provide complementary modulation without extra inversion.

behaviors.name: Difference rectifier
behaviors.description: The bottom outputs combine the three Z outputs into rectified positive and negative signals.

patchIdeas.name: Slow mod bank
patchIdeas.patch: Torpor X to filter cutoff · Apathy Y to VCA CV · Inertia Z to oscillator FM · three different rates keep one voice moving without needing three separate LFO modules.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Triple Sloths v2:
* Torpor: mid
* Apathy: noon

Filter:
VCA:
VCO:
Output:

- Triple Sloths v2 (Torpor X) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- Triple Sloths v2 (Apathy Y) >> VCA (CV)
- Triple Sloths v2 (Inertia Z) >> VCO (FM)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> VCA (In)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Quantized drift
patchIdeas.patch: Apathy X to quantizer input · quantizer out to oscillator V/Oct · Torpor Z to filter cutoff · slow chaos stays melodic while the filter breathes independently.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Triple Sloths v2:
* Torpor: low
* Apathy: high

Quantizer:
VCO:
Filter:
Output:

- Triple Sloths v2 (Apathy X) >> Quantizer (In)
- Quantizer (Out) p> VCO (V/Oct)
- Triple Sloths v2 (Torpor Z) >> Filter (Cutoff)
- VCO (Out) -> Filter (In)
- Filter (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Rectified accent source
patchIdeas.patch: use the + output as a positive accent CV and the - output as a complementary decay CV · one chaos module becomes a two-sided dynamics controller for drums or plucks.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Triple Sloths v2:
* Apathy: noon
* Torpor: noon

Envelope:
VCA:
Drum:
Output:

- Triple Sloths v2 (+) >> Envelope (Decay)
- Triple Sloths v2 (-) >> VCA (CV)
- Drum (Out) -> VCA (In)
- Envelope (Out) >> VCA (CV)
- VCA (Out) -> Output (In)

patchIdeas.name: Stereo drift
patchIdeas.patch: Torpor X and Apathy Z to left/right filter cutoffs · both sides move at different rates · the rectifier outputs add a slow, asymmetric stereo push.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Triple Sloths v2:
* Torpor: low
* Apathy: low

Filter:
Output:

- Triple Sloths v2 (Torpor X) >> Filter (Cutoff L)
- Triple Sloths v2 (Apathy Z) >> Filter (Cutoff R)
- Filter (Out L) -> Output (L)
- Filter (Out R) -> Output (R)

patchIdeas.name: Self-modulating chaos
patchIdeas.patch: external LFOs into Apathy CV and Torpor CV · the circuit sometimes locks into periodic windows, then falls back into wandering motion · useful when the patch needs a slow but not static movement source.
patchIdeas.diagram:
Triple Sloths v2:
* Torpor: mid
* Apathy: mid

LFO:
Output:

- LFO (Out) >> Triple Sloths v2 (Apathy CV)
- LFO (Out) >> Triple Sloths v2 (Torpor CV)
- Triple Sloths v2 (Inertia Y) >> Output (In)
